claim: YOLO demand evidence dispatch API implements boundary mutation audit that blocks PRD readiness when full-tool agents modify target project files.

evidence.summary: buildBoundarySnapshot creates SHA256 file hashes of project tree excluding allowed write roots; diffBoundarySnapshots detects added/modified/deleted files
evidence.why: Boundary audit implementation shows file-level mutation detection using cryptographic hashes before/after agent execution

evidence.summary: Post-agent-execution boundary audit compares snapshots and sets result.boundary with project_mutation status and changes array
evidence.why: Shows boundary audit runs after all evidence agents complete and captures detected mutations in dispatch result

evidence.summary: Boundary mutations create BOUNDARY_PROJECT_MUTATION blockers that prevent PRD readiness; external research requests without external evidence also block
evidence.why: Demonstrates boundary violations directly block PRD readiness via runtimeBlockers merged into final readiness

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_AGENT_PROTOCOLS define explorer, cross-checker, and verifier roles with writes_code=false and result_schema validation
evidence.why: Evidence agent protocols explicitly declare read-only enforcement and structured result requirements

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues enforces project-scoped facts require evidence with repo-relative path locators; returns blockers for missing paths
evidence.why: Validates that evidence for existing-project claims must include concrete file paths, not just scope declarations

evidence.summary: inspectDemandPrdReadiness checks evidence agent completion, scope declarations, project path requirements, and agent conflicts
evidence.why: Central PRD readiness validation integrates evidence agent results, scope validation, and blocker generation

evidence.summary: buildProviderInvocation constructs provider commands with configurable tools, permissions, and sandboxes for Claude, Codex, and custom agents
evidence.why: Provider adapter shows how full-tool agents are invoked with configurable permission modes and tool restrictions

evidence.summary: Recent dispatch artifact shows boundary audit result: project_mutation=clean, allowed_write_roots=[.yolo/demand/evidence/dispatch-*], changes=[]
evidence.why: Runtime evidence confirms boundary audit executed and detected no mutations in actual dispatch

evidence.summary: normalizeAgentResult validates evidence record scope declarations; sets EVIDENCE_SCOPE_REQUIRED blocker for invalid/missing scopes
evidence.why: Scope validation prevents agents from submitting evidence without declaring project/external/user/unknown classification

evidence.summary: buildDemandEvidenceAgentPrompt generates hard rules for agents: boundary forbids target project edits; missing must be [] not status text; evidence requires scope
evidence.why: Agent prompt generation shows boundary constraints and evidence schema requirements are enforced at protocol level

assumptions: Recent dispatch artifacts represent current codebase behavior; boundary audit may have evolved in earlier versions
Dirty working tree status does not affect boundary audit logic which uses git file snapshots
risks: If boundary snapshot misses new files not in git index, mutations could go undetected
If allowed_write_roots configuration is bypassed, agents could write outside artifact directories
If provider agent ignores permission mode or tool restrictions, boundary audit may catch mutation but PRD could be polluted

result.notes: Boundary mutation audit is implemented across buildBoundarySnapshot, diffBoundarySnapshots, and runtime blocker creation. PRD readiness integrates boundary violations as blockers. Evidence scope validation enforces project facts require path locators. Recent dispatch artifact confirms audit runs cleanly with no mutations detected.
